message:
fuzz: wire pdu_stream_decode into the fuzz-crate manifest

Companion manifest change for commit 20a4a3da ("fuzz: add
pdu_stream_decode target for codec::Pdu::stream_decode"). The target
file `fuzz/fuzz_targets/pdu_stream_decode.rs` was committed, but the
two manifest edits that make it buildable did not make it into that
commit. Splitting them out into this follow-up so bisect on either
commit still leaves the fuzz crate in a compilable state (the target
file is `#![no_main]` and cargo-fuzz will not try to build targets
that have no `[[bin]]` stanza, so either commit landing alone is
safe).

Two edits, both additive:

1. `[dependencies]` gains
      codec = { path = "../frankenterm/codec" }

   `codec` is the WezTerm-derived mux-protocol crate vendored under
   `frankenterm/codec`. It was not previously a fuzz-crate dep, so
   this adds it. No existing target references `codec`, so no
   cross-target rebuild cost.

2. `[[bin]] name = "pdu_stream_decode"` stanza, pointing at the
   target file with `doc = false` matching the convention used by
   every other target in the manifest.

After both the fuzz target file (20a4a3da) and this manifest change
are in tree, `cd fuzz && cargo +nightly fuzz list` reports the new
target, and `cargo +nightly fuzz run pdu_stream_decode` builds and
runs it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -10,6 +10,7 @@ cargo-fuzz = true
 [dependencies]
 libfuzzer-sys = "0.4.10"
 frankenterm-core = { path = "../crates/frankenterm-core" }
+codec = { path = "../frankenterm/codec" }
 toml = "0.8.23"
 serde = { version = "1.0.228", features = ["derive"] }
 rusqlite = { version = "0.38.0", features = ["bundled", "backup", "functions"] }
@@ -64,5 +65,10 @@ name = "string_parsers"
 path = "fuzz_targets/string_parsers.rs"
 doc = false
 
+[[bin]]
+name = "pdu_stream_decode"
+path = "fuzz_targets/pdu_stream_decode.rs"
+doc = false
+
 [profile.release]
 debug = 1
